commands.command: git push origin main && git push origin v2.2.0
commands.summary: Published release commit 2b3b549e915bbe69264ac59401cf533d11cc6532 to main and pushed annotated tag v2.2.0; remote tag dereference resolves to the same commit.

Directly uploaded both exact validated artifacts to production PyPI using operator-held credentials because GitHub Actions publishing was billing-locked. No credential content was read into or stored in evidence.

Public PyPI metadata reports latest release 2.2.0, Python >=3.11, a non-yanked 70,343-byte wheel with SHA-256 a254a6b23af94d30118b53422082c7a1e52310e1e40c58e16c6cb3716fa8f3ba, and a non-yanked 73,193-byte sdist with SHA-256 f137400f74b0fb743cffcf8b28dbe5d24b6a1fe13fc37b75af977f9745d437b8. Both match the pre-upload evidence.
A clean virtual environment downloaded and installed RepoPact 2.2.0 from the public index; installed metadata reported version 2.2.0.

The public wheel bootstrapped a fresh valid repository and its packaged validator accepted it. An earlier exploratory call used unsupported flag --root for init and exited before creating state; the documented --target invocation passed.

ForgeLink commit 86f8d67c9ea8770f364b35d06a5c417a00f31815 replaced the interim git pin with repopact==2.2.0. Its commit/push gates passed governance, 221 renderer tests, and 202 of 203 Node tests with one opt-in live Twilio test skipped.

environment.publication_route: operator-authorized direct PyPI upload; GitHub Actions billing-locked
environment.ci_limitation: Direct publication restores package delivery but does not restore GitHub Actions or cross-platform CI coverage.
